zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Llywelyn ap Cadwgon于大约1293年出生在威尔士卡迪甘史爾时，他的父亲Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydifor25岁，母亲Hunydd Verch Llywelyn18岁。他和Ellyw II ferch Maredudd至少有6个儿子和4个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Cadwgon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cadogan
meanings: Welsh (Pembrokeshire and Glamorgan) and Irish (of Welsh origin): from the Old Welsh personal name Catgucaun, Catguocawn, modernized as Cadwgon. It is composed of cad ‘battle’ + gogawn ‘honour, glory’. See also Wogan . The surname is stressed, like its Welsh original, on the penultimate syllable, hence its occasional development to Duggan .

nameMeanings.name: Llywelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Llewelyn
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Llywelyn ap Cadwgon was born about 1293, in Cardiganshire, Wales, his father, Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydifor, was 25 and his mother, Hunydd Verch Llywelyn, was 18. He had at least 6 sons and 4 daughters with Ellyw II ferch Maredudd.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Llywelyn ap Cadwgon est né environ 1293, à Cardiganshire, Galles, son père, Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydifor, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Hunydd Verch Llywelyn, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles avec Ellyw II ferch Maredudd.

de.lifeSummary: Als Llywelyn ap Cadwgon um 1293 in Cardiganshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydifor 25 und seine Mutter Hunydd Verch Llywelyn 18. Er hatte mit Ellyw II ferch Maredudd mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Llywelyn ap Cadwgon è nato il circa 1293 nel Cardiganshire, Galles, il suo padre, Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydifor, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Hunydd Verch Llywelyn, aveva 18 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie con Ellyw II ferch Maredudd.

ja.lifeSummary: Llywelyn ap Cadwgonが約1293年にウェールズカーディガンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydiforは25歳，母Hunydd Verch Llywelynは18歳であった。彼はEllyw II ferch Mareduddとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Llywelyn ap Cadwgon(이)가 대략 1293년 웨일스 카디건실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydifor(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Hunydd Verch Llywelyn(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Ellyw II ferch Maredudd(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Llywelyn ap Cadwgon родился около 1293 г. в местности кардиганьшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Cadwgon ap Fychan Cydifor было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Hunydd Verch Llywelyn, 18. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери от Ellyw II ferch Maredudd.
